methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Kyutai is a growth-stage AI & Software company that has raised €300M to date, founded by Xavier Niel. The valuation landscape for frontier AI comprises a sparse comp universe: one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks. This narrow set reflects the nascent and heterogeneous nature of the sector.

Traditional peer-median triangulation proves inapplicable here. Within the 38 publicly tracked AI & Software companies, filtering for same-stage growth companies, adjacent-stage peers, and similar valuation tiers yielded insufficient datapoints—zero same-stage comps, one adjacent-stage comp, and zero similar-tier peers—falling below the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median benchmarking. Reliance on all-sector medians would introduce substantial distortion given the structural differences between frontier AI infrastructure, large-language-model developers, and downstream enterprise software.

Without sufficient peer density to support triangulation, valuation determination requires reliance on alternative methods not detailed in the provided data—likely cost-of-capital approaches, precedent transaction analysis of the limited M&A activity in the space, or forward revenue multiple analysis if such metrics are available. The key uncertainties center on appropriate discount rates for pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier AI, comparability of business models across the heterogeneous comp set, and the stage-appropriate exit multiples in this nascent category. A defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the peer set alone.
